originalTitle: Bluey to be available in an Australian Indigenous language for the first time
summary: Five episodes have been dubbed into Australian language Yolŋu Matha for Naidoc Week, with Bandit voiced by a founding member of rock band King Stingray
content: It’s already been translated into 120 languages around the world but soon the globally loved children’s show Bluey will be available in an Australian Indigenous language for the first time. Five episodes from seasons one and two have been dubbed into Yolŋu Matha, the Australian First Nations language of North-east Arnhem Land, as part of an initiative for National Aboriginal and Islanders Day Observance Committee (Naidoc) Week. Due to be broadcast and streamed for the first time on Sunday 5 July, episodes The Beach, The Creek, Sleepytime, Grandad and Rug Island have been re-voiced by Dimathaya Burrawanga from the band King Stingray (as Bandit), Yolŋu educator and translator Rosie Mununggurr (as Chilli) and Andrew Gurruwiwi as the granddad. The children voicing Bluey and Bingo, who haven’t been named to protect their privacy, are local children from North-east Arnhem Land. The project was developed with Yolŋu Radio and the Aboriginal Resource and Development Services (ARDS), in collaboration with Bluey creators Ludo Studio and the ABC. Kelly Williams, the ABC’s director of First Nations strategy, said the Indigenous language episodes will “bring the North-east Arnhem Land language and culture into homes across the country”. “To hear these stories told in Yolŋu Matha, languages spoken on this continent for thousands of generations, is incredibly special,” said Julia Wormer and Sylvia Nulpinditj, co-chief executive officers at ARDS. “We hope this project contributes to a future where First Nations languages are increasingly heard, celebrated and recognised as a vital part of Australia’s story.” Bluey, Bingo, Bandit and Chilli have been streamed into millions of homes since the show launched in 2018. It is available in more than 140 countries and the animated series has topped the US streaming charts for the past two years. Naidoc Week runs from 5 to 12 July. Its theme is 50 Years of Deadly, honouring the strength, leadership and resilience of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples and communities. The Indigenous language episodes of Bluey will also be screened at this year’s Garma festival in August.
chineseTitle: Bluey 首次以澳大利亚本土语言推出
chineseSummary: Naidoc Week 的五集已被配音成澳大利亚语 Yolŋu Matha，其中 Bandit 由摇滚乐队 King Stingray 的创始成员配音
chineseContent: 它已被翻译成世界各地 120 种语言，但很快全球喜爱的儿童节目《Bluey》将首次以澳大利亚土著语言推出。作为全国原住民和岛民日纪念委员会 (Naidoc) 周活动的一部分，第一季和第二季中的五集已被配音为 Yolŋu Matha（阿纳姆地东北部的澳大利亚原住民语言）。 《The Beach》、《The Creek》、《Sleepytime》、《Grandad》和《Rug Island》剧集将于 7 月 5 日星期日首播，由 King Stingray 乐队的 Dimathaya Burrawanga（饰演 Bandit）、Yolŋu 教育家兼翻译家 Rosie Mununggurr（饰演 Chilli）和 Andrew Gurruwiwi 饰演爷爷重新配音。为 Bluey 和 Bingo 配音的孩子是来自阿纳姆地东北部的当地孩子，为了保护隐私，他们没有透露姓名。该项目是由 Yolŋu Radio 和原住民资源与开发服务 (ARDS) 与 Bluey 创作者 Ludo Studio 和 ABC 合作开发的。美国广播公司原住民战略总监凯利·威廉姆斯表示，土著语言剧集将“将东北阿纳姆地的语言和文化带入全国各地的家庭”。 ARDS 联合首席执行官 Julia Wormer 和 Sylvia Nulpinditj 表示：“听到用 Yolŋu Matha（这片大陆上数千代人使用的语言）讲述这些故事是非常特别的。” “我们希望这个项目能够为未来做出贡献，让原住民语言越来越多地被听到、庆祝并被认为是澳大利亚故事的重要组成部分。”自 2018 年推出以来，《Bluey》、《Bingo》、《Bandit》和《Chilli》已进入数百万家庭。该动画系列在 140 多个国家/地区播放，收视率最高。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
